title: ICARUS_VPI_CONST
body:
Using vpi_user.h with c++11 generates warnings as follows:

```
vpi.cpp:395:5: warning: ISO C++11 does not allow conversion from string literal to 'PLI_BYTE8 *' (aka 'char *') [-Wwritable-strings]
    VPI_REGISTER_FUNC(avr_create);
    ^
vpi.cpp:137:25: note: expanded from macro 'VPI_REGISTER_FUNC'
    tf_data.user_data = "$" #name;              \
                        ^
```

I see there was a discussion about const correctness in 2010 and the introduction of ICARUS_VPI_CONST but it is unclear how to use this correctly. Adding `-DICARUS_VPI_CONST=const` to `CXXFLAGS` doesn't work because it is redefined to nothing in vpi_user.h.

Is there a way to use ICARUS_VPI_CONST short of changing the define in vpi_user.h?

Perhaps change vpi_user.h to:
```
#ifndef ICARUS_VPI_CONST
#define ICARUS_VPI_CONST
#endif
```
so c++11 can compile without warnings?
code_blocks:
vpi.cpp:395:5: warning: ISO C++11 does not allow conversion from string literal to 'PLI_BYTE8 *' (aka 'char *') [-Wwritable-strings]
    VPI_REGISTER_FUNC(avr_create);
    ^
vpi.cpp:137:25: note: expanded from macro 'VPI_REGISTER_FUNC'
    tf_data.user_data = "$" #name;              \
                        ^
